applicability.sex_or_reproductive_context: patient with ovarian/uterine or pregnancy-capable reproductive physiology relevant to the complaint
use_when: Use when menstrual, ovulatory, androgen-excess, ovarian insufficiency, menopause, or pregnancy-exclusion context changes testing and counseling.
Use when local history confirms the reproductive physiology or anatomy relevant to the selected module.
do_not_use_when: Do not use as a generic endocrine workup when menstrual or ovarian physiology is not clinically applicable.
Do not use when pregnancy, postpartum state, prior hysterectomy/oophorectomy, or gender-affirming care changes the appropriate pathway without documenting that modifier.

requiredExam.label: Inspect acne distribution

source.source_section: Amenorrhea (Absence of menstruation) curated endocrine workup: required bedside maneuver
source.implementation_notes: Guideline-backed endocrine workup content curated into the local knowledge schema; schema, source, PHI, and regression tests run on 2026-06-06.

requiredExam.action: Use this maneuver to evaluate gonadal axis, prolactin, androgen excess/deficiency, or reproductive endocrine phenotype. Inflammatory acne supports hyperandrogenism and changes androgen testing threshold.
requiredExam.rationale: Targets gonadal axis, prolactin, androgen excess/deficiency, or reproductive endocrine phenotype; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Amenorrhea (Absence of menstruation).
requiredExam.technique: Inspect the relevant androgen-sensitive areas respectfully and document distribution, severity, and signs of virilization.
requiredExam.when_to_perform: When androgen excess or PCOS is possible
requiredExam.diagnostic_target: gonadal axis, prolactin, androgen excess/deficiency, or reproductive endocrine phenotype
requiredExam.likelihood_ratio_note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
requiredExam.management_change: Inflammatory acne supports hyperandrogenism and changes androgen testing threshold.
requiredExam.limitations: Inspect acne distribution is supportive for Amenorrhea (Absence of menstruation), not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.

requiredExam.label: Inspect thyroid gland

source.source_section: Amenorrhea (Absence of menstruation) curated endocrine workup: required bedside maneuver
source.implementation_notes: Guideline-backed endocrine workup content curated into the local knowledge schema; schema, source, PHI, and regression tests run on 2026-06-06.

requiredExam.action: Use this maneuver to evaluate thyroid structural disease, compressive features, or thyroid inflammatory/autoimmune pattern. Goiter changes thyroid testing interpretation and differential diagnosis.
requiredExam.rationale: Looks for localizing or mass-effect findings that change imaging, specialty escalation, or procedure planning for Amenorrhea (Absence of menstruation).
requiredExam.technique: Inspect and palpate the thyroid from in front or behind while the patient swallows, noting size, tenderness, asymmetry, nodules, fixation, or bruit if auscultated.
requiredExam.when_to_perform: When menstrual, fertility, or menopausal symptoms could reflect thyroid disease
requiredExam.diagnostic_target: thyroid structural disease, compressive features, or thyroid inflammatory/autoimmune pattern
requiredExam.likelihood_ratio_note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
requiredExam.management_change: Goiter changes thyroid testing interpretation and differential diagnosis.
requiredExam.limitations: Inspect thyroid gland is supportive for Amenorrhea (Absence of menstruation), not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique. Pair this technique-dependent finding with the relevant labs, imaging, serial trend, or specialty exam before changing definitive therapy.

requiredExam.label: Measure blood pressure

source.source_section: Amenorrhea (Absence of menstruation) curated endocrine workup: basic bedside data and safety checks
source.implementation_notes: Guideline-backed endocrine workup content curated into the local knowledge schema; schema, source, PHI, and regression tests run on 2026-06-06.

requiredExam.action: Check and document as baseline bedside data for this workup. Hypotension or severe hypertension changes acuity, medication safety, fluid strategy, and escalation.
requiredExam.when_to_perform: Perform when Amenorrhea (Absence of menstruation) is the selected validated workup and the maneuver is clinically safe and relevant at bedside.
requiredExam.diagnostic_target: hemodynamic safety, hypertension or hypotension severity, and medication or fluid-safety context
requiredExam.management_change: Hypotension or severe hypertension changes acuity, medication safety, fluid strategy, and escalation.
requiredExam.limitations: Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.
requiredExam.likelihood_ratio_note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
requiredExam.technique: Measure blood pressure using an appropriately sized cuff after a brief rest period, seated or supine as clinically appropriate.
requiredExam.rationale: Targets hemodynamic safety, hypertension or hypotension severity, and medication or fluid-safety context; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Amenorrhea (Absence of menstruation).

requiredExam.limitations: Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.
requiredExam.likelihood_ratio_note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

requiredExam.label: Determine mental status

source.implementation_notes: Guideline-backed endocrine workup content curated into the local knowledge schema; schema, source, PHI, and regression tests run on 2026-06-06.

requiredExam.action: Check and document as baseline bedside data for this workup. Altered mental status changes urgency, disposition, and need for monitored evaluation.
requiredExam.when_to_perform: Perform when Amenorrhea (Absence of menstruation) is the selected validated workup and the maneuver is clinically safe and relevant at bedside.
requiredExam.diagnostic_target: encephalopathy, crisis severity, or airway-protection risk
requiredExam.management_change: Altered mental status changes urgency, disposition, and need for monitored evaluation.
requiredExam.limitations: Global appearance and perfusion are subjective and affected by baseline function, pain, medications, temperature, and examiner experience; pair with objective vitals and reassessment.
requiredExam.likelihood_ratio_note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
requiredExam.technique: Determine mental status using a brief bedside assessment of alertness, orientation, and responsiveness.
requiredExam.rationale: Screens severity and immediate safety findings that can change triage, monitoring, fluids, or urgent escalation for Amenorrhea (Absence of menstruation).

conditionalExam.label: Inspect for clitoromegaly

source.source_section: Amenorrhea (Absence of menstruation) curated endocrine workup: conditional bedside maneuver add-ons
source.implementation_notes: Guideline-backed endocrine workup content curated into the local knowledge schema; schema, source, PHI, and regression tests run on 2026-06-06.

conditionalExam.action: Add when virilization, rapid, androgen, hirsutism is present; this evaluates gonadal axis, prolactin, androgen excess/deficiency, or reproductive endocrine phenotype. Virilization changes urgency of androgen tumor evaluation.
conditionalExam.rationale: Targets gonadal axis, prolactin, androgen excess/deficiency, or reproductive endocrine phenotype; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Amenorrhea (Absence of menstruation).
conditionalExam.technique: Inspect the relevant androgen-sensitive areas respectfully and document distribution, severity, and signs of virilization.
conditionalExam.when_to_perform: When rapid virilization or severe androgen excess is present
conditionalExam.diagnostic_target: gonadal axis, prolactin, androgen excess/deficiency, or reproductive endocrine phenotype
conditionalExam.likelihood_ratio_note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
conditionalExam.management_change: Virilization changes urgency of androgen tumor evaluation.
conditionalExam.limitations: Inspect for clitoromegaly is supportive for Amenorrhea (Absence of menstruation), not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.

conditionalExam.label: Inspect breast tissue

conditionalExam.action: Add when gynecomastia, breast mass, nipple discharge, breast is present; this evaluates gonadal axis, prolactin, androgen excess/deficiency, or reproductive endocrine phenotype. Suspicious breast findings change endocrine versus breast-mass pathway.
conditionalExam.rationale: Targets gonadal axis, prolactin, androgen excess/deficiency, or reproductive endocrine phenotype; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Amenorrhea (Absence of menstruation).
conditionalExam.technique: Inspect and palpate breast tissue to distinguish glandular tissue from adiposity and to identify discrete mass, tenderness, or discharge.
conditionalExam.when_to_perform: When gynecomastia, breast mass, or nipple symptoms are present
conditionalExam.diagnostic_target: gonadal axis, prolactin, androgen excess/deficiency, or reproductive endocrine phenotype
conditionalExam.likelihood_ratio_note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
conditionalExam.management_change: Suspicious breast findings change endocrine versus breast-mass pathway.
conditionalExam.limitations: Inspect breast tissue is supportive for Amenorrhea (Absence of menstruation), not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.

conditionalExam.label: Inspect mucous membranes for dehydration

conditionalExam.action: Add when vomiting, dehydration, poor intake, polyuria, hyperglycemia is present; this evaluates volume status, dehydration severity, or peripheral perfusion. Dehydration changes fluid strategy, lab urgency, and monitoring needs.
conditionalExam.rationale: Targets volume status, dehydration severity, or peripheral perfusion; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Amenorrhea (Absence of menstruation).
conditionalExam.technique: Inspect oral mucosa and tongue moisture with attention to dry mucosa, cracked lips, and overall hydration context.
conditionalExam.when_to_perform: When vomiting, poor intake, polyuria, hyperglycemia, hypercalcemia, or hypernatremia is present
conditionalExam.diagnostic_target: volume status, dehydration severity, or peripheral perfusion
conditionalExam.likelihood_ratio_note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
conditionalExam.management_change: Dehydration changes fluid strategy, lab urgency, and monitoring needs.
conditionalExam.limitations: Inspect mucous membranes for dehydration is supportive for Amenorrhea (Absence of menstruation), not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.

reference_values: Prolactin upper limit often about 20 ng/mL in men and 25 ng/mL in women, but lab, pregnancy, lactation, and macroprolactin matter.
Male total testosterone should be early morning and repeated; lower limit often around 264-300 ng/dL depending assay.
IGF-1, LH/FSH, estradiol, AMH, and pediatric growth labs require age/sex/puberty-specific ranges.
TSH typical adult range about 0.4-4.0 mIU/L; use local lab, pregnancy, and illness context.
Free T4 typical adult range about 0.8-1.8 ng/dL; use local assay.
T3, TRAb/TSI, TPO Ab, and thyroglobulin Ab are assay-specific.
Use local laboratory intervals and assay-specific cutoffs; endocrine immunoassays are affected by pregnancy, age, sex, acute illness, binding proteins, biotin, and medication effects.
decision_steps: Rule out pregnancy first in all pregnancy-capable patients with amenorrhea.
Initial labs include TSH, prolactin, FSH, and estradiol; add androgen testing when hyperandrogenism is present.
Use FSH/estradiol pattern to separate ovarian insufficiency from hypothalamic/pituitary causes, then image pituitary or pelvis when indicated.
Check urgent causes: pregnancy with pain/bleeding, severe eating disorder/vital instability, headache/vision symptoms, or rapid virilization.
Confirm that Amenorrhea (Absence of menstruation) is the selected clinical intent, then screen for red flags before routine outpatient testing.
Order the first-line tests in sequence: Pregnancy test first; TSH and prolactin; FSH and estradiol.

Interpret numeric results against the workup cutoffs and local assay ranges: Prolactin upper limit often about 20 ng/mL in men and 25 ng/mL in women, but lab, pregnancy, lactation, and macroprolactin matter. Male total testosterone should be early morning and repeated; lower limit often around 264-300 ng/dL depending assay.

If the initial result is equivocal, discordant with the phenotype, or affected by medication, acute illness, pregnancy, age, sex, or assay interference, repeat or confirm using the next guideline test before committing to long-term therapy.
treatment_options: Treat the cause: pregnancy care, thyroid disease, hyperprolactinemia, PCOS/anovulation, functional hypothalamic amenorrhea, POI, or structural outflow disorder.
For functional hypothalamic amenorrhea, prioritize nutrition, weight restoration, exercise reduction, stress care, and bone protection.
For POI, provide hormone replacement when appropriate, fertility counseling, and bone/cardiovascular prevention.
Provide endometrial protection for chronic anovulation when pregnancy is not desired.
Treat unstable presentations first using the urgent escalation rule for Amenorrhea (Absence of menstruation); draw time-sensitive labs only if doing so does not delay stabilization.
For confirmed Amenorrhea (Absence of menstruation), choose therapy based on severity, comorbidities, pregnancy/fertility context, contraindications, patient preference, and local specialty pathway.
Monitor treatment response with the condition-specific clinical endpoint plus the most relevant labs or imaging listed in this workup.
Escalate to endocrinology or the relevant specialty when results are severe, discordant, recurrent, treatment-limiting, or require localization, surgery, genetic testing, or high-risk medication.
